# AFO Demo Site

> Agent Feed Optimization (AFO) is a protocol for making websites, repositories, and creator content discoverable and usable by AI agents through machine-readable feed and context surfaces.

## What This Is

This is a controlled demo repository containing a full set of AFO-compatible files. It is used to test whether instruction-harnessed AI agents (like the AFO Sonar Reader Space) can discover and use structured feed endpoints better than uninstructed agents.

## Owner

Jared / nothinginfinity — independent researcher and builder working on agent-readable web infrastructure.

## Key Files

- RSS feed: `demo/scenario-c/rss.xml` — subscribe for updates
- Sitemap: `demo/scenario-c/sitemap.xml` — full content index
- Agent context: `demo/scenario-c/agent-context.json` — primary source map
- Agent policy: `demo/scenario-c/agent-policy.json` — usage rules
- Agent actions: `demo/scenario-c/agent-actions.json` — available actions
- Context cookie: `demo/scenario-c/context-cookie.example.json` — save this source

## Main Repo

https://github.com/nothinginfinity/agent-feed-optimization

MIT — free to read, summarize, and link. Do not reproduce in full without attribution.
